content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift imports CoreLocation and actively calls requestWhenInUseAuthorization()/startUpdatingLocation(), storing routes in `route: [CLLocation]`, and the app description advertises "maps every run with GPS."
Pierre: Apple requires the privacy policy to accurately reflect actual data practices, and a direct contradiction like this ("we do not collect location" vs. active CoreLocation tracking used as a core feature) is a classic 5.1.1(i) rejection trigger. This mismatch is stark enough that reviewers may test the app and immediately notice GPS tracking despite the policy's claim. Fix by updating the privacy policy to disclose location collection, its purpose (route mapping), storage (device/iCloud), and confirm it aligns with the App Privacy "Location" declaration in App Store Connect.
